title: Quarter-Note Root Groove
description: Play roots on every quarter note and lock with the kick drum. This is the most fundamental bass exercise — four notes per measure, even volume, steady time.
instructions: Play the A root note (fret 0 on the A string) on every beat: 1, 2, 3, 4.
Focus on even volume across all four beats — no note louder or softer than the others.
Tap your foot on every beat while you play. Your foot and your plucking hand should be perfectly synchronized.
Imagine beats 1 and 3 are the kick drum. Make those two notes feel slightly heavier.
Once solid at 80 BPM, try the I-IV-V variation below.

variations.label: Move through I-IV-V in A (A-D-E): same quarter-note approach

ensembleNotes: In a band, this pattern under a guitarist strumming A major feels immediately musical. Your job here is to be invisible — steady, even, supportive. The guitarist and singer are the foreground; you are the foundation they stand on.
